From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

One of the several Negro baseball leagues in the USA

West Coast Negro Baseball Association

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Classification | Minor league |
| Sport | [Negro league baseball](/wiki/Negro_league_baseball "Negro league baseball") |
| Founded | 1946 |
| Ceased | 1946 |
| No. of teams | 6 |
| Country | [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") |

The **West Coast Negro Baseball Association**[[1]](#cite_note-1) (**WCNBA**) was one of the several [Negro baseball leagues](/wiki/Negro_league_baseball "Negro league baseball") created during the time organized baseball was segregated. The WCNBA was organized as a minor league in 1946 by [Abe Saperstein](/wiki/Abe_Saperstein "Abe Saperstein") and [Jesse Owens](/wiki/Jesse_Owens "Jesse Owens") as a means to provide the [west coast](/wiki/West_Coast_of_the_United_States "West Coast of the United States") with a platform for [African-American](/wiki/African-American "African-American") players. The league lasted about three months.

## League history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=West_Coast_Negro_Baseball_Association&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: League history")]

During [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II") in the mid-1940s, the African American population grew as wartime jobs demanded workers to relocate to the shipyards and military institutions along the California, Oregon and Washington coasts. After the war ended in 1945, Eddie Harris and David P. Portlock began the process of organizing a Negro league in the area using the current minor league [Pacific Coast League](/wiki/Pacific_Coast_League "Pacific Coast League") (PCL) stadiums while those teams were on the road.[[2]](#cite_note-OAC-2) They reached out to Abe Saperstein to assist. Saperstein already owned a barnstorming team called the Cincinnati Crescents, which he moved to Seattle and renamed them the [Seattle Steelheads](/wiki/Seattle_Steelheads "Seattle Steelheads"). He knew [Jesse Owens](/wiki/Jesse_Owens "Jesse Owens"), the track and field athlete and four-time Olympic gold medalist, and asked him to join. Owens formed the [Portland Rosebuds](/wiki/Portland_Rosebuds_(baseball) "Portland Rosebuds (baseball)") in Oregon.[[3]](#cite_note-SABR-3) Saperstein was elected league president, Owens vice-president and Portlock secretary.[[2]](#cite_note-OAC-2) A 110-game season was planned. However, the league survived for only about three months. Most teams played under 30 games and the league folded due to "poor attendance, a lack of financing, and difficulty in accessing ballparks."[[3]](#cite_note-SABR-3) Seattle and Oakland continued the season playing local teams across the midwest.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## League franchises

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=West_Coast_Negro_Baseball_Association&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: League franchises")]

The league was to be made up of six teams in cities with PCL stadiums. Originally, a team was slated to play in [Fresno](/wiki/Fresno "Fresno"), a non-PCL city. They moved their base of operations to San Diego, but did initially play at least one game in Fresno. Oakland far outpaced the rest of the league in the shortened season, with Seattle and San Francisco finishing above .500. San Diego, Portland and Los Angeles rounded out the standings.[[5]](#cite_note-CNLBR-5)

| WCNBA Team | Home field[[6]](#cite_note-6) | PCL counterpart |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [Los Angeles White Sox](/wiki/Los_Angeles_White_Sox "Los Angeles White Sox") | [Wrigley Field](/wiki/Wrigley_Field_(Los_Angeles) "Wrigley Field (Los Angeles)") | ([Los Angeles Angels](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Angels_(PCL) "Los Angeles Angels (PCL)")) |
| [Oakland Larks](/wiki/Oakland_Larks "Oakland Larks") | [Oakland Park](/wiki/Oakland_Baseball_Park "Oakland Baseball Park") | ([Oakland Oaks](/wiki/Oakland_Oaks_(PCL) "Oakland Oaks (PCL)")) |
| [Portland Rosebuds](/wiki/Portland_Rosebuds_(baseball) "Portland Rosebuds (baseball)") | [Vaughn Street Park](/wiki/Vaughn_Street_Park "Vaughn Street Park") | ([Portland Beavers](/wiki/Portland_Beavers "Portland Beavers")) |
| [Fresno-San Diego Tigers](/wiki/San_Diego_Tigers "San Diego Tigers") | Fresno Midget Auto Racing Park [Lane Field](/wiki/Lane_Field_(baseball) "Lane Field (baseball)") | ([San Diego Padres](/wiki/San_Diego_Padres_(PCL) "San Diego Padres (PCL)")) |
| [San Francisco Sea Lions](/wiki/San_Francisco_Sea_Lions "San Francisco Sea Lions") | [Seals Stadium](/wiki/Seals_Stadium "Seals Stadium") | ([San Francisco Seals](/wiki/San_Francisco_Seals_(baseball) "San Francisco Seals (baseball)")) |
| [Seattle Steelheads](/wiki/Seattle_Steelheads "Seattle Steelheads") | [Sick's Stadium](/wiki/Sick%27s_Stadium "Sick's Stadium") | ([Seattle Rainiers](/wiki/Seattle_Rainiers "Seattle Rainiers")) |
